name: Soupa Patsas (Tripe Soup)
recipeInstructions:
1. Clean, scrape, and wash the tripe and feet. Scald in boiling water; cool.
2. Cut the tripe into small pieces and place in a large pot with the feet. Cover with plenty of water.
3. Bring to a boil, skimming off the froth as it rises. Lower the heat.
4. Add the garlic, and cook at a slow boil for 1 to 2 hours, adding a little coarse salt just before the meat is tender.
5. Remove from the heat; strain the broth and reserve. Discard the bones from the feet.
6. Cut the meat into pieces and return to the broth.
7. Prepare the avgolemono: Beat the eggs very well. Slowly add the lemon juice, beating constantly.
8. Add a little hot broth from the pot, stirring continuously, then pour the mixture back into the soup.
9. Add pepper just before serving.
10. Note: This soup can be served not only hot but also cold. For cold serving, let it set and cut into squares or diamonds. When served hot, some prefer mixing with oil and vinegar instead of avgolemono.
